ny work
on prosody. The two verses, viz., 5 and 13, ofXLVII have not been
named, though they come under the Pankti group. He resorts also
to grafting of one metre on another and thereby creates a new
metre : Verse 14 of XLVII has been effected by combining one foot
the Rathoddhata (of eleven syllables) and the other of Priyarhvadd
(of twelve syllables). This has resulted in the creation of a new
Ardhasama metre. In LXXXV, 6 he combines one of Vdtormi with an-
other of Sdlini\ in LXXXVIII verses 19, 21 and 27 are in Bhadravtrdf
minus the last long syllable; verse 26 of XLVII is of a nameless
metre, but it is a variety of Upasthitd where instead of the penulti-
mate long syllable there is a short one; in XXXIII, 29 the metre
cannot be identified; verse 31 of LXXXVIII is an amalgam of
Samdnikd and Pramdnikd; verse 12 of LXIX is in an unidentified
metre, but in a footnote of the Pingalacchandas the author of the
note £ri kr$na byname calls it Snigdhd metre; he uses Prdkrta metre in
LXX.
